pages.name: Home
pages.description: Homepage introducing the precision CNC machining manufacturer and directing users to key tools and content.
primary_functions: Summarize core CNC machining capabilities, industries served (aerospace, medical, etc.), and quality certifications.
Provide prominent calls-to-action to Request a Quote, Cost Estimator, Lead Time Calculator, Capabilities, Equipment, and Standard Parts catalog.
Highlight featured case studies and services to help users quickly navigate to relevant examples and offerings.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Services' in header navigation or services CTA
Click on 'Request a Quote' in header navigation or primary hero CTA
Click on Capabilities primary action
Click on Equipment primary action
Click on Standard Parts catalog primary action
Click on Cost Estimator primary action
Click on Lead Time Calculator primary action
Click on Case Studies section or navigation
Click on About Us in header/footer
Click on Cart icon in header
Click on a featured service card
Click on a featured case study card

access_methods.description: Main 'Home' navigation entry
Footer 'Home' link
Users can land directly on the homepage via the root URL or index.html

pages.name: Services Overview
pages.description: High-level overview of machining services offered by the company.
primary_functions: Present main service categories such as CNC turning, 3-axis machining, 5-axis machining, prototyping, and production runs with brief descriptions.
Link users into detailed capabilities listings and service detail pages for deeper technical specifications.
Offer a clear call-to-action to the Request a Quote form for custom parts.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a service category tile to view detailed capabilities for that process
Click the 'Request a Quote' CTA from Services Overview

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Services' in the main header navigation
Linked from homepage feature sections and other internal content

pages.name: Capabilities & Processes
pages.description: Filterable listing of machining capabilities and services with technical specifications.
primary_functions: Allow users to filter services by process (e.g., 5-axis cnc machining), material (e.g., aluminum, titanium, stainless steel), tolerance, part size, and industry.
Provide sorting options such as tolerance tightest-first or newest capabilities first.
Display a list of matching services with key specs and link each entry to its Service Detail page.

incoming_params.description: Optional initial process filter (e.g., 'five_axis_cnc_machining') applied when arriving from Services or homepage CTAs.
Optional initial material filter (e.g., 'aluminum_6061').
Optional industry filter (e.g., 'aerospace', 'medical').
Optional maximum tolerance in mm used to pre-populate the tolerance filter.
Optional maximum tolerance in inches used to pre-populate the tolerance filter.
Optional sort option (e.g., 'tolerance_tightest', 'newest').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a specific service entry in the capabilities listing

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Capabilities' in the main header navigation
Filters and sort options can be initialized via query parameters (e.g., capabilities.html?process_type=five_axis_cnc_machining)
Linked from Services, Home, and other internal content blocks

pages.name: Service Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific machining service or capability.
primary_functions: Present detailed information about the service including supported materials, achievable tolerances, maximum part size, surface finish capabilities, typical lead times, and example applications.
Show related example projects or case studies relevant to this service where appropriate.
Provide a prominent 'Contact Us about this Service' button that takes the user to the Service Inquiry form pre-associated with this service.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the Service to display details for.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Contact Us about this Service' button
Click on a related case study teaser within the Service Detail page

access_methods.description: Accessed as service_detail.html?service_id=<SERVICE_ID>
Linked from Capabilities listing, Home featured services, and Case Studies where relevant

pages.name: Service Inquiry
pages.description: Contact form specifically tied to a selected machining service.
primary_functions: Allow users to submit questions or requests related to a specific service, including a subject line and detailed message.
Prepopulate or clearly display the referenced service so users and staff understand which capability the inquiry concerns.
Collect basic contact details (name, email, optional phone/company) and submit the inquiry.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the Service the inquiry refers to; used to preconfigure the inquiry form.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to Service Details' link from the inquiry page

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'Contact Us about this Service' links as service_inquiry.html?service_id=<SERVICE_ID>
The form itself is submitted via submitServiceInquiry without changing page

pages.name: Request a Quote
pages.description: Comprehensive RFQ form for custom machined parts.
primary_functions: Enable users to specify machining type (e.g., CNC turning, milling), material (e.g., Stainless Steel 304), dimensions (length, diameter), quantity, and tolerance options.
Provide lead-time selection options including standard and expedited choices, showing typical business-day windows.
Allow users to attach drawings or CAD files and add notes or requirements (e.g., inspections, finishes).
Collect contact information (name, email, phone, company) and submit the RFQ.
Accept prefilled inputs from other tools (e.g., Cost Estimator preferred material) to streamline RFQ creation.

incoming_params.description: Optional CostEstimate id used to prefill material, quantity, and surface finish in the RFQ form.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to Cost Estimator' link to adjust cost assumptions

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Request a Quote' in the main header navigation
Accessible via 'Request a Quote' footer link
When coming from Cost Estimator, accessed as request_quote.html?preferred_cost_estimate_id=<ID>
Linked from multiple CTAs on Home, Services, and other pages

pages.name: Cost Estimator
pages.description: Interactive tool to estimate per-unit cost for different materials and finishes.
primary_functions: Allow users to select part type (e.g., medical implant/component), quantity, material (e.g., Titanium Grade 5, Stainless Steel 316L), and surface finish level.
Calculate and display estimated cost per unit for the selected configuration.
Support rapid comparison by letting users change only the material or finish and recalculate costs while keeping other inputs constant.
Provide a control to mark one configuration (material) as the preferred option and proceed directly to the Request a Quote page with that selection carried over.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Proceed to Quote' after marking a configuration as Preferred

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Resources – Cost Estimator' in the main header navigation
Accessible via 'Cost Estimator' footer link
Linked from homepage CTAs and RFQ workflow guidance

pages.name: Case Studies
pages.description: Listing of customer case studies and success stories across industries.
primary_functions: Allow users to filter case studies by industry (e.g., aerospace, medical, industrial) and by delivery time or lead-time tags (e.g., 10 days or less).
Offer sorting such as fastest delivery first or most recent to surface relevant examples quickly.
Display summaries of each case study including industry, part type, processes used, and delivery time.
Link each summary to a full Case Study Detail page.

incoming_params.description: Optional initial industry filter (e.g., 'aerospace', 'medical').
Optional lead-time tag filter (e.g., 'le_10_days').
Optional sort selection (e.g., 'fastest_delivery', 'most_recent').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a case study title or thumbnail

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Case Studies' in the main header navigation
Accessible via 'Case Studies' footer link
Filters and sorting can be initialized via query parameters

pages.name: Case Study Detail
pages.description: Detailed write-up of a specific case study.
primary_functions: Present full narrative, project objectives, technical challenges, processes used, materials, tolerances, and achieved delivery time.
Show photos or diagrams of parts and highlight measurable results (e.g., lead-time reduction, cost savings).
Offer a 'Start a Similar Project' or equivalent button that leads to the Similar Project Request form with this case study referenced.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the case study to display in full detail.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Start a Similar Project' CTA
Click 'Back to Case Studies' link

access_methods.description: Accessed as case_study_detail.html?case_study_id=<CASE_STUDY_ID>
Linked from Case Studies listing and featured case studies on the homepage

pages.name: Similar Project Request
pages.description: Project request form for users who want work similar to a specific case study.
primary_functions: Display a project name field where users can reference the chosen case study (e.g., 'Aerospace project similar to [case study title]').
Provide a description/message field for users to state that they want a project similar to the case study and specify target delivery times (e.g., 10 days or less).
Collect contact details such as name and email and submit the request for follow-up.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the case study the user wants to emulate.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View Case Study Again' link from the Similar Project Request page

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'Start a Similar Project' buttons as similar_project_request.html?case_study_id=<CASE_STUDY_ID>
The request form is submitted via submitSimilarProjectRequest

pages.name: Equipment Listing
pages.description: Catalog of CNC machines and equipment with detailed capabilities.
primary_functions: List all CNC machines and key equipment with core specs such as axes, maximum part dimensions, achievable tolerances, and surface finish capability.
Provide filters including minimum part length (e.g., 6 500 mm) and maximum surface roughness (e.g., 6 Ra 1.6 �b5m), plus optional process or machine-type filters.
Offer sort options like newest first or most capable first.
Allow users to select multiple machines via 'Add to Compare' controls and then open a comparison view for the chosen machines.

incoming_params.description: Optional minimum part length filter to initialize the listing (e.g., 500).
Optional maximum Ra surface roughness filter (e.g., 1.6).
Optional machine type filter (e.g., 'five_axis_mill').
Optional machine sorting option (e.g., 'newest_first', 'most_capable_first').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Compare (X)' or 'View Comparison' bar after selecting machines

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Equipment' in the main header navigation
Accessible via 'Equipment' footer link
Filter and sort state can be initialized via query parameters

pages.name: Equipment Comparison
primary_functions: Display a side-by-side comparison table of selected machines, highlighting key specs including maximum part size, surface finish capability, tolerance, axes, and other relevant parameters.
Allow users to review and confirm that machines meet requirements such as handling parts 6 500 mm and finishing to 6 Ra 1.6 �b5m.
Provide an option to save or email the comparison (e.g., 'Email Comparison to Myself') by entering an email address and sending a summary while keeping the user on the page.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the EquipmentComparison to display; if omitted, falls back to the current active comparison.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to Equipment List' from the comparison page

access_methods.description: Accessed as equipment_compare.html?comparison_id=<COMPARISON_ID>
Opened via 'Compare (X)' control on the Equipment Listing page

pages.name: About Us
primary_functions: Share information about the companys history, mission, certifications, quality systems, and core expertise in precision CNC machining.
Highlight key industries served and any differentiators such as in-house inspection, rapid prototyping, or specialized materials.
Provide a clear link or call-to-action to the 'Visit Our Facility' page for users interested in plant tours.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on 'Visit Our Facility' / 'Plant Tour' CTA
Click on an aerospace-focused case studies link within About Us

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'About Us' in the main header navigation
Accessible via 'About Us' footer link
Can be visited directly via about.html URL

pages.name: Visit Our Facility
pages.description: Information and scheduling for plant tours and facility visits.
primary_functions: Describe visit guidelines, safety requirements, available tour types, and what visitors can expect to see (e.g., CNC machining cells, inspection labs).
Present a 'Schedule a Tour' or 'Request a Visit' form including a date picker for selecting a weekday in the next month, time-of-day options (e.g., morning), number of attendees, and notes field.
Collect visitor contact information (name, email, optional phone) and submit tour requests.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to About Us' link from Visit Our Facility page

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Visit Our Facility' footer link
Linked from About Us and potentially from Home for plant tour CTAs
Plant tour form is submitted via submitPlantTourRequest without leaving the page

pages.name: Products Catalog
pages.description: Standard parts catalog for purchasable CNC turning and milling components.
primary_functions: Display a catalog of standard parts organized by category such as CNC Turning Parts and CNC Milling Parts.
Provide filters for category and maximum price (e.g., limit to items under $150), along with basic search if needed.
Show product cards with name, price, and key attributes, each linking to a Product Detail page and/or offering quick add-to-cart where appropriate.

incoming_params.description: Optional initial category filter (e.g., 'cnc_turning_parts', 'cnc_milling_parts').
Optional maximum price filter (e.g., 150).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a product card to view details
Click on header 'Cart' or 'View Cart' link from the catalog

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Standard Parts' in the main header navigation
Accessible via 'Standard Parts Catalog' footer link
Category and price filters can be initialized via query parameters

pages.name: Product Detail
pages.description: Detailed view of a specific standard part.
primary_functions: Present full specifications for the part including dimensions, material, finish, tolerances, and pricing.
Allow users to set a quantity and add the part to the cart.
Suggest related parts or compatible items where useful.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the catalog product to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View Cart' after adding the product to cart
Click 'Back to Category' from Product Detail page

access_methods.description: Accessed as product_detail.html?product_id=<PRODUCT_ID>
Linked from Products Catalog and potentially from featured product sections

pages.name: Cart
pages.description: Cart and review page for selected standard parts.
primary_functions: List all items currently in the users cart, including part name, category (turning/milling), quantity, unit price, and line total.
Allow users to update quantities or remove items as needed.
Display subtotal and any relevant comments about next steps (e.g., proceed to checkout or convert cart items into a quote request).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Continue Shopping' from the Cart page
Click 'Request a Quote for These Items' CTA, if provided

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Cart' in the main header navigation
Accessible via 'Cart' footer link
Linked from Add-to-Cart confirmations and product pages

pages.name: Lead Time Calculator
pages.description: Tool to estimate total lead time based on quantity, processes, and shipping options.
primary_functions: Allow users to input quantity (e.g., 500 parts) and select additional processes such as anodizing and dimensional inspection.
Calculate and display available production plus shipping options (e.g., Standard, Expedited, Express) with total business-day lead times and relative costs.
Enable users to select an option that meets a target total lead time (e.g., 6 12 business days), prioritizing the lowest-cost qualifying option.
Provide a summary section on the same page showing the chosen quantity, selected processes, and selected shipping/service level after the user clicks Continue or View Summary.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Request a Quote with This Lead Time' CTA, if offered

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Lead Time Calculator' in the main header navigation
Accessible via 'Lead Time Calculator' footer link
Linked from Home and RFQ-related guidance

header_links.description: Homepage with overview of CNC precision machining capabilities and key tools.
Overview of machining services offered with links to detailed capabilities.
Custom RFQ form for precision CNC machined parts.
Filterable listing of machining capabilities and service offerings.
CNC machine and equipment list with filters and comparison tools.
Catalog of standard CNC turning and milling parts.
Cost estimator tool to compare per-unit costs for different materials and finishes.
Lead-time and shipping options calculator for machined parts.
Industry case studies and success stories with filters by industry and lead time.
Company background, quality systems, and facility overview.
View and manage items currently in the shopping cart.

footer_links.description: Learn more about the company and its capabilities.
Information and form to schedule a plant tour.
Submit an RFQ for custom machining work.
Estimate and compare part costs by material and finish.
Calculate total production and shipping lead times.
Browse CNC machines and their capabilities.
Read customer success stories and project examples.
Browse standard CNC turning and milling parts.
Review items you have added to your cart.
